runs.notes: size not recorded; completed before /dev/shm exhaustion forced cleanup

analysis: zstd3 is the right default: pays 25% more time than raw for 34% better compression.
zstd9 is a poor trade: 57% more time for only 4% smaller files. Classic ZSTD diminishing returns above level 3.
snappy saves 23% time over zstd3 but typically produces ~30% larger files.
none is a legitimate option when storage is cheap and regeneration is frequent: 28% time saving at 46% size inflation over zstd3.
